sections.heading: RT
sections.content:
Principles:
- Consider definitive RT for patients with active localized disease.
- Consider adjuvant RT after lymph node dissection if disease is limited to a single nodal site with extranodal extension or inadequate nodal dissection with multiple positive nodes.
- Consider palliative RT for symptomatic patients (e.g., uncontrolled pain, impending pathologic fracture, cord compression).
- Stereotactic body RT (SBRT) or stereotactic ablative radiotherapy (SABR) can be considered for limited (1–3) metastases and pulmonary metastases.

Dose Frameworks:
- Adjuvant RT for nodal basin: total dose 45 Gy, dose per fraction 1.8–2.0 Gy, fractions 25–28, daily 5 days/week. Indication: After lymph node dissection for isolated supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal nodal metastasis with extranodal extension or inadequate dissection. May include a boost of 5–9 Gy.
- SBRT/SABR for oligometastases: total dose 48–60 Gy, dose per fraction 10–15 Gy, fractions 4–5, daily or every other day. Indication: Limited (1–3) metastases and pulmonary metastases.
- Palliative RT hypofractionation: 8 Gy in 1 fraction, or 20 Gy in 4–5 fractions, or 30 Gy in 10 fractions. Indication: Symptomatic metastases (e.g., pain, impending fracture, cord compression).

Approaches:
- Intensity-Modulated Radiation Therapy (IMRT): dose fractionation as per standard dose frameworks; allows for conformal dose delivery. May be used with concurrent systemic therapy. Indication: For improved dose conformity, particularly for head and neck, pelvic, and other complex sites. Key trials: Retrospective studies show promising locoregional control and survival. Toxicities: Mucositis, xerostomia, dysphagia, neck stiffness, trismus.

timeline.title: Adjuvant RT for nodal basin
timeline.body: Total dose: 45 Gy, fractions: 25–28, schedule: Daily, 5 days/week. Indication: After lymph node dissection for isolated nodal metastasis with extranodal extension or inadequate dissection. May include boost of 5–9 Gy.

timeline.title: SBRT/SABR for oligometastases
timeline.body: Total dose: 48–60 Gy, fractions: 4–5, schedule: Daily or every other day. Indication: Limited (1–3) metastases and pulmonary metastases.

timeline.title: Palliative RT hypofractionation
timeline.body: Options: 8 Gy in 1 fraction, or 20 Gy in 4–5 fractions, or 30 Gy in 10 fractions. Indication: Symptomatic metastases (e.g., pain, impending fracture, cord compression).
